items.title: [FEA] Enable auto-generation of world model API documentation from individual node channel definitions, state schemas, and pub/sub topic metadata
items.body:
## Description
从个体节点频道定义、状态 schema 和 pub/sub topic 元数据自动生成世界模型 API 文档。工具链在构建期解析 IndividualNode 注册宏与 StateSchema 声明，输出结构化 Markdown/HTML 文档，消除手写文档与代码的同步负担。

```cpp
// 声明即文档
WORLD_INDIVIDUAL_NODE(
    IndividualId = "robot_arm_01",
    /// @brief 机械臂末端位置状态
    /// @pub  /world/robot_arm/position  [IndividualPos]
    /// @sub  /world/control/command     [ArmCommand]
    channels = {
        PUB("/world/robot_arm/position", IndividualPos),
        SUB("/world/control/command",    ArmCommand),
    }
);
// -> 自动生成 API 文档
```

## Dependencies
- world 注解宏解析工具（Clang AST 插件或自定义预处理器）
- StateSchema 序列化元数据
- Sphinx / Doxygen 后端集成

## Sub-tasks
- [ ] 实现 WORLD_INDIVIDUAL_NODE 宏的 AST 解析器，提取频道元数据
- [ ] 设计 StateSchema 文档注解格式（字段名、类型、单位、范围）
- [ ] 生成 Markdown 格式 API 文档（频道列表、状态字段说明）
- [ ] 集成到 CMake 构建：build 阶段自动触发文档生成
- [ ] CI 验证：文档生成失败时阻断合并

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 所有 WORLD_INDIVIDUAL_NODE 注册的频道自动出现在文档中
- [ ] StateSchema 字段类型、单位、范围正确渲染
- [ ] 文档与代码同步：修改频道定义后文档自动更新
- [ ] 生成文档无手写内容，通过 diff 验证与代码一致
- [ ] CMake 集成后构建时间增加 < 10s

items.title: Migrate tools/codegen.cpp string formatting to std::format for type-safe protobuf message stub generation in the pub/sub-loop build pipeline
items.body:
## Description
tools/codegen.cpp 中 protobuf 消息存根（stub）生成使用 sprintf/字符串拼接，存在格式字符串类型不安全和缓冲区溢出风险。迁移至 std::format，提供类型安全的格式化，并消除手动缓冲区管理。

```cpp
// 当前（不安全）
char buf[256];
sprintf(buf, "struct %s { %s fields; };",
    msg_name.c_str(), fields_str.c_str());  // 缓冲区溢出风险

// 迁移后（类型安全）
auto stub = std::format(
    "struct {} {{ {} fields; }};",
    msg_name, fields_str
);
```

## Dependencies
- C++23 std::format（或 fmtlib 兼容层）
- tools/codegen.cpp 构建依赖
- pub/sub-loop CI 代码生成流水线

## Sub-tasks
- [ ] 审查 codegen.cpp 中所有 sprintf/printf/字符串拼接调用
- [ ] 逐一替换为 std::format 等价实现
- [ ] 消除手动 char buf[] 缓冲区，改用 std::string
- [ ] 验证生成的 protobuf 消息存根与迁移前完全一致
- [ ] CI 集成：codegen 输出 diff 检查，防止格式回归

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] codegen.cpp 无 sprintf/printf/字符串拼接残留
- [ ] 生成的 protobuf 存根与迁移前 diff 为空
- [ ] 编译无 -Wformat-security 警告
- [ ] CI codegen diff 检查通过
- [ ] 迁移后代码行数减少（更简洁）

items.title: [FEA] Define consolidation plan for unifying cyber:: sub-module namespaces under a coherent world:: hierarchy for pub/sub-loop
items.body:
## Description

@pub-sub-loop-common · P2 🟢 · FEA · Claude-B

### Problem

The pub/sub-loop codebase currently uses a fragmented namespace hierarchy inherited from the original Cyber RT platform. Individual state types, world utilities, and shared primitives are scattered across multiple sub-namespaces:

```cpp
// Current: fragmented namespaces
cyber::common::GlobalData        // world-level shared data
cyber::timer::Timer              // world clock / tick timer
cyber::time::Duration            // time duration for state TTL
cyber::base::Signal              // individual lifecycle signals
cyber::base::AtomicHashMap       // lock-free map for individual registry
cyber::event::EventManager       // individual event dispatch
cyber::croutine::RoutineFactory  // croutine creation helpers
```

This fragmentation causes several problems:

1. **Inconsistent discoverability**: A developer implementing a new individual component must guess whether a utility lives in `cyber::common`, `cyber::base`, `cyber::time`, or `cyber::event`. There is no single namespace to explore.

2. **Circular dependency risk**: `cyber::common` depends on `cyber::base` for `AtomicHashMap`, and `cyber::base` depends on `cyber::common` for `GlobalData`. The circular include is currently broken by forward declarations, but it is fragile.

3. **Name collision with world model concepts**: The `cyber::` prefix is confusing when the project is called pub/sub-loop. New developers expect `pubsub::` or `world::` prefixes.

4. **Migration friction**: Every new module that touches shared utilities must add 3-5 `using namespace` declarations or fully-qualified names, adding 10-20 LOC of boilerplate per file.

### Proposed consolidation

Unify all shared primitives under a two-level namespace: `world::` for world-model concepts, `world::std` for standard-library-like utilities:

```
Consolidation map:

  cyber::common::GlobalData       → world::GlobalState
  cyber::common::Environment      → world::Environment
  cyber::common::Macros           → world::macros (header-only, no namespace)
  
  cyber::timer::Timer             → world::TickTimer
  cyber::timer::TimingWheel       → world::TimingWheel
  
  cyber::time::Duration           → world::std::Duration
  cyber::time::Rate               → world::std::Rate
  cyber::time::Timestamp          → world::std::Timestamp
  
  cyber::base::Signal             → world::Signal
  cyber::base::AtomicHashMap      → world::std::AtomicHashMap
  cyber::base::BoundedQueue       → world::std::BoundedQueue
  cyber::base::ObjectPool         → world::std::ObjectPool
  cyber::base::ThreadPool         → world::std::ThreadPool
  cyber::base::WaitStrategy       → world::std::WaitStrategy
  
  cyber::event::EventManager      → world::EventBus
  cyber::event::PerfEventCache    → world::PerfEventCache
  
  cyber::croutine::RoutineFactory → world::CRoutineFactory
```

### Migration strategy

Phase 1: **Alias layer** (non-breaking)
```cpp
// world/compat/cyber_aliases.h
// Generated automatically — provides backward-compatible aliases
namespace cyber::common {
    using GlobalData = world::GlobalState;  // [[deprecated("use world::GlobalState")]]
}
namespace cyber::base {
    using Signal = world::Signal;  // [[deprecated]]
    using AtomicHashMap = world::std::AtomicHashMap;  // [[deprecated]]
}
// ... all 30+ aliases
```

Phase 2: **Mechanical migration** (module-by-module)
```bash
# Automated find-and-replace per module, validated by CI
sed -i 's/cyber::common::GlobalData/world::GlobalState/g' module/src/*.cc
sed -i 's/cyber::base::Signal/world::Signal/g' module/src/*.cc
# ... per-symbol replacement scripts
```

Phase 3: **Remove aliases** (after all modules migrated)
- Delete `world/compat/cyber_aliases.h`
- Remove `cyber::` namespace declarations from all headers

### Dependency graph (post-consolidation)

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                   world::                        │
│                                                  │
│  ┌──────────────┐  ┌──────────────┐             │
│  │ GlobalState   │  │ Environment  │             │
│  │ Signal        │  │ EventBus     │             │
│  │ TickTimer     │  │ TimingWheel  │             │
│  │ CRoutineFactory│ │ PerfEventCache│            │
│  └──────┬───────┘  └──────┬───────┘             │
│         │                  │                     │
│  ┌──────▼──────────────────▼──────┐              │
│  │          world::std            │              │
│  │  Duration, Rate, Timestamp     │              │
│  │  AtomicHashMap, BoundedQueue   │              │
│  │  ObjectPool, ThreadPool        │              │
│  │  WaitStrategy                  │              │
│  └────────────────────────────────┘              │
│         (no upward dependencies)                 │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

`world::std` has zero dependencies on `world::` — it is a leaf layer. `world::` depends only on `world::std`. This eliminates the circular dependency.

### Impact assessment

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Files to modify | ~380 (.cc and .h across all modules) |
| Symbols to rename | 32 types + 15 free functions |
| Estimated mechanical migration time | 2 hours (scripted) |
| Deprecation period for aliases | 2 sprints |
| Risk of behavioral change | Zero (pure rename, no logic changes) |

## Dependencies

- Blocks: nothing (alias layer is additive)
- Blocked by: nothing (can start immediately)
- Affects: every module that uses `cyber::common`, `cyber::base`, `cyber::time`, `cyber::event`

## Sub-tasks

1. Define complete symbol mapping table (all 47 symbols) — **P1**
2. Create `world/` header directory structure — **P1**
3. Implement Phase 1 alias layer with `[[deprecated]]` annotations — **P1**
4. Write migration scripts for Phase 2 — **P2**
5. Migrate modules one-by-one: transport → scheduler → data → component → node → ... — **P2**
6. CI gate: fail build if any non-deprecated `cyber::` reference exists — **P3**
7. Phase 3: remove alias layer after all modules pass CI — **P3**

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All shared primitives accessible via `world::` or `world::std::` namespace
- [ ] `world::std` has zero dependencies on `world::` (verified by include-what-you-use)
- [ ] Alias layer compiles with deprecation warnings, not errors
- [ ] Full CI passes after each module migration (no regression)
- [ ] Zero `cyber::common::` or `cyber::base::` references remain after Phase 3
- [ ] Generated documentation reflects new namespace structure

items.title: [FEA] tools::CI Windows NVRTC — 新增 Windows 平台 NVRTC 运行时编译与 pub/sub 集成测试 CI 流水线
items.body:
## Description
在 CI 流水线中新增 Windows 平台的 NVRTC（世界运行时 JIT 编译）集成测试，覆盖 Windows 下个体状态处理内核的动态编译与 pub/sub 通道集成场景，确保跨平台一致性。

```yaml
# CI 配置（GitHub Actions / Jenkins）
jobs:
  windows-nvrtc-test:
    runs-on: windows-2022
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Setup CUDA Toolkit (Windows)
        uses: Jimver/cuda-toolkit@v0.2
        with: { cuda: '12.4.0' }
      - name: Build NVRTC integration tests
        run: cmake --build . --target world_nvrtc_tests
      - name: Run pub/sub NVRTC integration tests
        run: ctest -R "nvrtc_pubsub" --output-on-failure
```

## Dependencies
- Windows CUDA Toolkit >= 12.0
- NVRTC 头文件和运行时库
- world::RuntimeCompiler Windows 适配层

## Sub-tasks
- [ ] 配置 Windows CI 环境（CUDA Toolkit + MSVC/Clang-cl）
- [ ] 适配 world::RuntimeCompiler 的 Windows 路径处理
- [ ] 实现 NVRTC 编译缓存（避免每次 CI 重新编译内核）
- [ ] 新增 Windows 专属集成测试：pub/sub + JIT 内核
- [ ] CI 矩阵：Windows Server 2019/2022 两版本

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Windows CI 流水线成功构建 NVRTC 集成测试
- [ ] NVRTC JIT 编译在 Windows 下产生正确内核
- [ ] pub/sub + JIT 集成测试通过（Windows）
- [ ] 编译缓存命中率 >= 80%（避免重复编译）
- [ ] CI 运行时间 < 15 分钟（含缓存预热）

items.title: [FEA] 修复 data::StreamTaskFlow pub/sub-loop 通道管道的 MSVC 编译支持——消除 MSVC 特定的 C++ 语法不兼容
items.body:
## Description
`data::StreamTaskFlow` pub/sub-loop 通道管道当前在 MSVC 编译器下存在语法不兼容问题，包括：结构化绑定（structured bindings）的扩展用法、`if constexpr` 的部分模板特化，以及 `__attribute__` 扩展。需逐一修复，确保 MSVC 2022 下完整编译通过。

```cpp
// 问题1：MSVC 不支持 lambda 捕获的结构化绑定
auto [pub, sub] = create_channel<IndividualState>();
auto task = [pub = std::move(pub)]() { pub.send(state); };
// MSVC 错误：cannot capture structured binding

// 修复：显式命名
auto channel = create_channel<IndividualState>();
auto task = [pub = std::move(channel.publisher)]() { pub.send(state); };

// 问题2：__attribute__((always_inline)) -> __forceinline (MSVC)
#ifdef _MSC_VER
  #define WORLD_FORCEINLINE __forceinline
#else
  #define WORLD_FORCEINLINE __attribute__((always_inline)) inline
#endif
```

## Dependencies
- MSVC 2022 编译器
- Windows CI 流水线
- C++17/20 跨编译器兼容性指南

## Sub-tasks
- [ ] 修复结构化绑定 lambda 捕获问题（MSVC CWG 限制）
- [ ] 替换 `__attribute__` 为跨平台宏（WORLD_FORCEINLINE 等）
- [ ] 修复 MSVC 的 if constexpr 部分模板特化问题
- [ ] 新增 Windows/MSVC CI 编译任务
- [ ] 验证：StreamTaskFlow 在 MSVC 下完整编译并通过测试

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] data::StreamTaskFlow 在 MSVC 2022 下零 error 零 warning 编译
- [ ] 所有 __attribute__ 扩展替换为跨平台宏
- [ ] Windows CI 任务集成，首次通过
- [ ] MSVC 与 GCC/Clang 测试结果一致
- [ ] 跨平台兼容性指南文档更新

[BUG] Fix potentially uninitialized/OOB reads of vsmem in DeviceMergeSort, DeviceReduceByKey, and DeviceSelect for non-aligned individual state collections
items.body:
## Description

### 世界模型定位
`world::std` 模块缺陷修复：DeviceMergeSort / DeviceReduceByKey / DeviceSelect 在处理非对齐个体状态集合时，存在未初始化/越界读取 vsmem 的问题。

### 问题影响
在 pub/sub-loop 世界模型中，个体（individual）通过 Cyber RT pub/sub 通道持续发布位置、速度、属性等状态数据。当个体状态集合的内存对齐不满足 ISG（warp）级批量处理要求时，排序/归约/选择操作会读取未初始化的 vsmem 区域，导致：
- 个体状态排序结果不确定（影响 TopK 优先级选择）
- 归约聚合值被污染（影响多源状态融合精度）
- 选择操作遗漏或重复个体（影响世界视图一致性）

### 个体输出动态关联
此缺陷直接影响通过 needle-tools 渲染的个体动态输出——当状态排序或归约错误时，GLB 场景中的个体位置/属性会出现跳变或闪烁。

## Dependencies
- `world::std` 模块（DeviceMergeSort, DeviceReduceByKey, DeviceSelect）
- 个体状态内存对齐规范（`base::AlignedStateLayout`）
- ASAN/TSAN 内存安全工具

## Sub-tasks
- [ ] 构建最小复现用例（非对齐个体状态集合 + MergeSort）
- [ ] 修复 vsmem 初始化逻辑，对非 tile 对齐的尾部元素做 padding 填充
- [ ] 修复 ReduceByKey 对非对齐集合的边界条件处理
- [ ] 修复 DeviceSelect 的非对齐索引计算
- [ ] 边界条件测试：1/31/33/63/65/127/129 等非 2^n 对齐长度
- [ ] ASAN 验证：无越界读取，无未初始化内存访问

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 非对齐个体状态集合的排序/归约/选择结果与对齐版本完全一致
- [ ] ASAN/TSAN 检测通过，无内存安全问题
- [ ] 修复不引入额外性能开销（对齐集合路径不变）
- [ ] 回归测试覆盖所有非 2^n 对齐的边界情况
- [ ] 个体动态输出场景（needle-tools GLB 渲染）无跳变

items.body:
## Description

### 世界模型定位
`world::std` 模块功能需求：Migrate world::std::reduce_by_key to use scheduler::ReducePolicy backend for parallel individual state group aggregation

### pub/sub-loop 上下文
世界模型标准库——提供个体状态处理的基础算法原语（排序、归约、扫描、选择、变换），这些原语是 pub/sub 通道数据处理的核心构建块。本需求服务于世界模型的功能需求目标，确保个体（individual）在世界中持续更新数据和位置时，相关功能正确、高效、可靠。

### 个体输出动态关联
common 模块的算法直接决定个体状态的计算精度和处理吞吐量，影响最终通过 needle-tools 渲染时个体动态的流畅度和一致性。

## Dependencies
- `world::std` 模块
- pub/sub 通道基础设施
- 个体状态类型系统（message-safe, standard_layout）

## Sub-tasks
- [ ] 需求分析与接口设计
- [ ] 核心实现（Mixed）
- [ ] 单元测试与边界条件覆盖
- [ ] 集成测试（pub/sub 端到端验证）
- [ ] 文档更新

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 功能符合 world:: 命名空间规范
- [ ] 与 pub/sub-loop 个体状态处理管道集成正确
- [ ] 通过 ASAN/TSAN 内存安全检查
- [ ] 性能不低于基线（profiler 基准验证）
- [ ] 个体动态输出场景验证通过

items.title: [EPIC] Port all std-equivalent parallel algorithms from tensor_parallel:: to world::std:: namespace with scheduler backend dispatch
items.body:
## Description

@pub-sub-loop-common · P3 · EPIC · Claude-B

### Overview

The common module contains a set of `std`-equivalent parallel algorithms in the `tensor_parallel` namespace (`for_each`, `transform`, `reduce`, `scan`, `sort`, `unique`, `partition`, `merge`, `find`, `count`, `equal`). These operate on individual state collections sequentially on the host CPU. They should be ported to the `world::std` namespace and re-implemented to dispatch through `scheduler::StateProcessPolicy`, which provides parallel execution on world processing units (IPUs).

### Migration scope

```
tensor_parallel → world::std migration inventory:

ALREADY PORTED:
  tensor_parallel::min_element     → world::std::min_element     (common PRD #4)
  tensor_parallel::max_element     → world::std::max_element     (common PRD #4)
  tensor_parallel::minmax_element  → world::std::minmax_element  (common PRD #4)
  tensor_parallel::erase           → world::std::erase           (transport PRD #19)
  tensor_parallel::erase_if        → world::std::erase_if        (transport PRD #19)
  tensor_parallel::reduce_by_key   → world::std::reduce_by_key   (common PRD #13)

TO PORT (this EPIC):
  tensor_parallel::for_each        → world::std::for_each
  tensor_parallel::transform       → world::std::transform
  tensor_parallel::reduce          → world::std::reduce
  tensor_parallel::inclusive_scan   → world::std::inclusive_scan
  tensor_parallel::exclusive_scan  → world::std::exclusive_scan
  tensor_parallel::sort            → world::std::sort
  tensor_parallel::stable_sort     → world::std::stable_sort
  tensor_parallel::unique          → world::std::unique
  tensor_parallel::unique_by_key   → world::std::unique_by_key
  tensor_parallel::partition       → world::std::partition
  tensor_parallel::merge           → world::std::merge
  tensor_parallel::find            → world::std::find
  tensor_parallel::find_if         → world::std::find_if
  tensor_parallel::count           → world::std::count
  tensor_parallel::count_if        → world::std::count_if
  tensor_parallel::equal           → world::std::equal
  tensor_parallel::copy            → world::std::copy
  tensor_parallel::copy_if         → world::std::copy_if
  tensor_parallel::fill            → world::std::fill
```

### Design pattern (same for all algorithms)

```cpp
namespace world::std {

// Each algorithm follows this delegation pattern:
template <typename Range, typename Op>
auto for_each(Range&& individuals, Op op) {
    // 1. Detect execution context
    if constexpr (is_device_accessible<Range>()) {
        // Individual states are on world processing device → parallel execution
        return scheduler::StateProcessPolicy<typename Range::value_type>
            ::for_each(std::forward<Range>(individuals), op);
    } else {
        // Host-side fallback → standard library
        return ::std::for_each(individuals.begin(), individuals.end(), op);
    }
}

}  // namespace world::std
```

### Priority order

Port in order of usage frequency in the codebase:

| Algorithm | Call sites | Priority |
|-----------|-----------|----------|
| `for_each` | 47 | P0 |
| `transform` | 38 | P0 |
| `sort` | 31 | P0 |
| `reduce` | 28 | P0 |
| `inclusive_scan` | 22 | P1 |
| `exclusive_scan` | 19 | P1 |
| `copy` / `copy_if` | 18 | P1 |
| `find` / `find_if` | 15 | P1 |
| `unique` / `unique_by_key` | 12 | P2 |
| `count` / `count_if` | 11 | P2 |
| `merge` | 8 | P2 |
| `partition` | 6 | P2 |
| `fill` | 5 | P3 |
| `equal` | 3 | P3 |
| `stable_sort` | 2 | P3 |

## Dependencies

- Depends on: `scheduler::StateProcessPolicy` for each algorithm variant
- Depends on: common PRD #1 (namespace consolidation) for `world::std` namespace
- Blocks: removal of `tensor_parallel` namespace (common cleanup EPIC)

## Sub-tasks

1. Port P0 algorithms (for_each, transform, sort, reduce) — **P0**
2. Port P1 algorithms (scan, copy, find) — **P1**
3. Port P2 algorithms (unique, count, merge, partition) — **P2**
4. Port P3 algorithms (fill, equal, stable_sort) — **P3**
5. Add `[[deprecated]]` to all `tensor_parallel::` originals — **P2**
6. Comprehensive correctness tests against sequential reference — **P1**

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All 19 algorithms available in `world::std::` namespace
- [ ] Parallel execution for device-accessible data, host fallback otherwise
- [ ] Results are identical to `tensor_parallel::` versions for all test cases
- [ ] `tensor_parallel::` aliases are deprecated but functional during migration period
- [ ] ≥5x speedup over sequential for individual collections of 10K+ elements

# [FEA] Add Clang-21 Cross-Compilation Support to CI for Tools Module World Inspector CLI

## Summary
Extend the CI pipeline to validate that the `tools` module (world inspector CLI, state recorder, individual monitor) compiles cleanly under clang-21. This ensures forward compatibility with the latest C++23/26 features used in the world model codebase and validates the embedded toolchain path for aarch64 targets.

## Motivation
The `tools` module provides critical operational utilities: `cyber_monitor` (individual state viewer), `cyber_recorder` (world state recording), and `cyber_channel` (pub/sub channel inspector). These must compile on the latest compilers to catch deprecation warnings, leverage new standard library features (e.g., `std::expected`, `std::generator`), and ensure the aarch64 cross-compilation path works for RK3568 deployment.

## Requirements

### Functional Requirements
- CI matrix addition: clang-21 for x86_64 and aarch64 cross-compilation
- All `tools` module targets must build without warnings under `-Wall -Wextra -Werror`
- Runtime tests for `cyber_monitor` and `cyber_recorder` must pass under clang-21 built binaries
- Validate that clang-21's coroutine support works with `croutine`-based tool plugins

### Non-Functional Requirements
- CI job should complete in < 10 minutes
- Should not block PRs on clang-21 failures initially (advisory mode for 2 sprints)
- Docker image with clang-21 toolchain published to project registry

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CI config updated with clang-21 matrix entry
- [ ] `tools` module builds clean under clang-21 (x86_64)
- [ ] Cross-compilation to aarch64 succeeds
- [ ] `cyber_monitor --list-individuals` test passes on clang-21 binary
- [ ] Advisory mode flag documented for team

## Design Notes
- Use the existing CI template and add clang-21 as a matrix dimension
- May need to patch `std::format` usage if clang-21 libc++ diverges from gcc's libstdc++
- Coordinate with `common` module for any compiler-specific `#ifdef` paths

## Dependencies
- `tools` module source
- `common` module (compiler abstraction macros)
- `croutine` module (coroutine support)
- CI infrastructure (Docker, GitHub Actions)

## Cyber RT Module Mapping
**tools** — CLI tools for world inspection (monitor, recorder, etc)

Enable `<world/individual>` APIs to be used within Individual Processing Units (IPU)
items.body:
## Background

In the pub/sub-loop world model, each **individual** runs within its own Individual Processing Unit (IPU), analogous to a thread running on a GPU streaming multiprocessor. The `<world/individual>` header provides core APIs for IPU-local operations: reading an individual's own state, publishing updates to its pub/sub channel, querying its group-shared state cache, and interacting with its tick scheduler.

Currently, these APIs are **host-only**: they can only be called from the world runtime orchestration layer (host side), not from within the individual's own execution context (IPU side). This severely limits individual autonomy—an individual cannot call world-model APIs on itself during its own state-update kernel execution.

## Problem Analysis

### Current State

```cpp
// world/individual.h
// All APIs currently marked host-only
namespace world {
  // ERROR: cannot call this from within an individual's own execution
  WORLD_HOST_ONLY
  void individual_publish(IndividualHandle h, StateUpdate update);

  WORLD_HOST_ONLY
  StateSnapshot individual_read_state(IndividualHandle h);

  WORLD_HOST_ONLY
  void individual_subscribe(IndividualHandle h, TopicId topic);
}
```

When an individual's state-processing kernel executes, it cannot call `world::individual_publish()` or `world::individual_read_state()` directly. It must relay everything through host-side callbacks, creating unnecessary latency and architectural friction.

### Root Cause

The `<world/individual>` APIs use host-only intrinsics internally (memory-mapped I/O, OS sync primitives). These need to be replaced or wrapped with IPU-compatible equivalents that work within the individual's execution context.

### Impact
- Individual state-update logic cannot be self-contained
- Composite individuals (e.g., a transformer individual composed of encode + decode sub-individuals) cannot coordinate internally without host intervention
- Tick-gated dispatch cannot be managed by the individual itself
- Significant latency overhead from host round-trips

## Design

### API Annotation Strategy

Introduce dual-context annotation macros:

```cpp
// world/execution_context.h
#define WORLD_IPU_CALLABLE   __attribute__((world_ipu_callable))
#define WORLD_HOST_CALLABLE  __attribute__((world_host_callable))
#define WORLD_UNIVERSAL      __attribute__((world_ipu_callable, world_host_callable))

// Replaces WORLD_HOST_ONLY for APIs that can run in both contexts
#define WORLD_DUAL_CONTEXT   WORLD_UNIVERSAL
```

### Refactored `<world/individual>` APIs

```cpp
// world/individual.h
namespace world {

// State read: safe in both host and IPU context
WORLD_DUAL_CONTEXT
inline StateSnapshot individual_read_state(IndividualHandle h) {
  #if defined(WORLD_IPU_CONTEXT)
    return ipu_intrinsic::load_individual_state(h.ipu_state_ptr);
  #else
    return host_runtime::fetch_state_snapshot(h);
  #endif
}

// Publish: IPU context uses lock-free pub channel
WORLD_DUAL_CONTEXT
inline void individual_publish(IndividualHandle h, StateUpdate update) {
  #if defined(WORLD_IPU_CONTEXT)
    ipu_intrinsic::pub_channel_write(h.pub_channel, update);
  #else
    host_runtime::enqueue_publish(h, update);
  #endif
}

// Subscribe: only makes sense from host context (topology config)
WORLD_HOST_CALLABLE
void individual_subscribe(IndividualHandle h, TopicId topic);

// New IPU-only: read group-shared state cache
WORLD_IPU_CALLABLE
GroupStateCache& individual_group_cache(IndividualHandle h);

} // namespace world
```

### Composite Individual Interaction Protocol

For composite individuals (e.g., transformer = encode sub-individual + decode sub-individual), IPU-callable APIs enable direct sub-individual coordination without host round-trips:

```
+------------------------------------------+
|         Transformer Individual           |
|  +----------------+  +----------------+ |
|  | Encode Sub-IPU |->| Decode Sub-IPU | |
|  | (WORLD_IPU API)|  | (WORLD_IPU API)| |
|  +----------------+  +----------------+ |
|         |                    |           |
|    pub_channel          pub_channel      |
+------------------------------------------+
         |                    |
    World Pub/Sub Bus (RTPS/DDS)
```

### Context Detection

```cpp
// world/context_detect.h
#if defined(__WORLD_IPU_ARCH__)
  #define WORLD_IPU_CONTEXT 1
#else
  #define WORLD_HOST_CONTEXT 1
#endif
```

## Dependencies

- `world::ipu_intrinsic` low-level IPU memory access layer
- `world::host_runtime` state snapshot service
- CMake toolchain update for `__WORLD_IPU_ARCH__` detection
- `<world/execution_context.h>` annotation header (new)

## Sub-tasks

- [ ] Define `WORLD_DUAL_CONTEXT` / `WORLD_IPU_CALLABLE` / `WORLD_HOST_CALLABLE` macros in `<world/execution_context.h>`
- [ ] Implement `ipu_intrinsic::load_individual_state()` and `ipu_intrinsic::pub_channel_write()`
- [ ] Refactor `individual_read_state()` and `individual_publish()` with dual-context dispatch
- [ ] Add `individual_group_cache()` IPU-only API
- [ ] Update CMake toolchain to detect and define `__WORLD_IPU_ARCH__`
- [ ] Write unit tests: call `<world/individual>` APIs from within a simulated IPU execution context
- [ ] Test composite individual (encode+decode) intra-communication without host round-trips
- [ ] Update documentation and migration guide for previously host-only callers

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All `WORLD_DUAL_CONTEXT` APIs compile and run correctly in both host and IPU execution contexts
- [ ] An individual's state-update kernel can call `individual_publish()` and `individual_read_state()` directly
- [ ] Composite individuals (encode+decode sub-individuals) can exchange state via IPU-callable APIs without host intervention
- [ ] No regression in existing host-side callers of `<world/individual>` APIs
- [ ] CI passes for both host-context and IPU-context test suites
- [ ] Latency benchmark shows >30% reduction in intra-composite individual communication vs. host round-trip baseline

[BUG] Fix CLI option parsing failure in tools module JIT individual-type compiler
items.body:
# [BUG] Fix CLI Option Parsing Failure in Tools Module JIT Individual-Type Compiler

## Summary
Fix a bug in the `tools` module's JIT (just-in-time) individual type compiler where CLI option parsing fails for certain flag combinations. The JIT compiler allows developers to dynamically compile and hot-reload individual types without restarting the world, and broken option parsing prevents this workflow.

## Bug Description
When invoking the JIT individual-type compiler via the `cyber_jit` CLI tool with combined short flags (e.g., `-vO2` instead of `-v -O2`), the option parser fails with an unrecognized option error. This also affects the `--include-path` flag when the path contains special characters (spaces, colons for cross-compilation sysroots).

### Steps to Reproduce
1. `cyber_jit compile --individual-type MyCustomIndividual -vO2 --include-path /opt/rk3568-sysroot/usr/include`
2. Error: `Unrecognized option: -vO2`
3. Expected: verbose mode ON, optimization level O2

### Root Cause
The option parser uses a simple prefix-match that doesn't handle combined short flags. Additionally, `--include-path` parsing splits on `:` which conflicts with Windows-style paths and cross-compilation sysroot paths.

## Requirements

### Fix Scope
- Update CLI option parser to handle combined short flags correctly
- Fix `--include-path` parsing to support paths with colons and spaces (quote-aware)
- Add `--sysroot` flag for cross-compilation (separate from include paths)
- Preserve backward compatibility with existing flag formats

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Combined short flags parsed correctly (`-vO2`, `-vfPIC`)
- [ ] Include paths with spaces and colons work
- [ ] `--sysroot` flag works for RK3568 cross-compilation
- [ ] Existing CI tests pass without modification
- [ ] New regression tests added for edge cases

## Design Notes
- Consider migrating to a proper CLI parsing library (e.g., CLI11, argparse)
- The JIT compiler is part of the `tools` module and uses `class_loader` to dynamically load compiled `.so` files
- Hot-reload triggers a `plugin_manager` re-registration

## Dependencies
- `tools` module (JIT compiler)
- `class_loader` module (dynamic loading)
- `plugin_manager` module (re-registration)

## Cyber RT Module Mapping
**tools** — CLI tools for world inspection (monitor, recorder, etc)

Port `IndividualStateSync::minmax_element` to IndividualPipeline
items.body:
## Background

In pub/sub-loop, `IndividualStateSync` (原 `tensor_parallel`) provides collective operations for groups of individuals. `minmax_element` finds min/max state values across an individual group in a single pass.

Currently this only works in flat sync groups. It has not been ported to **IndividualPipeline** (`PipelineParallel`), which arranges individuals as producer→consumer stages. This blocks global min/max queries across all pipeline stages.

---

## Problem Analysis

### Current State

```cpp
// Only works in flat IndividualStateSync context
namespace world::std::individual_sync {
  template<typename StateT, typename IteratorT>
  std::pair<StateT, StateT> minmax_element(IteratorT first, IteratorT last);
}
// NOT available in IndividualPipeline context
```

### Why Pipeline Context is Different

In `IndividualPipeline`, individuals are divided into stages:

```
Stage 0 (producers)   Stage 1 (processors)   Stage 2 (consumers)
  ind_0, ind_1            ind_2, ind_3            ind_4, ind_5
  state: [3, 7]           state: [1, 9]           state: [2, 6]

Desired: global minmax = (1, 9) across all 6 individuals
Current: per-stage only, requires manual cross-stage reduction
```

### Problems

1. No cross-stage reduction: `minmax_element` cannot aggregate across pipeline stages
2. Synchronization gap: pipeline stages run asynchronously; naive port breaks throughput
3. Iterator incompatibility: pipeline uses stage-partitioned iterators; flat sync assumes contiguous range
4. Partial results: if a stage is mid-flight when called, result is undefined

### Impact

- Cannot compute global min/max across a running pipeline
- Pipeline-aware load balancing algorithms are not implementable

---

## Proposed Design

### Two-Phase Pipeline MinMax

```
Phase 1: Per-stage local minmax (concurrent across all stages)
  Stage 0: local_min=3, local_max=7
  Stage 1: local_min=1, local_max=9
  Stage 2: local_min=2, local_max=6

Phase 2: Cross-stage reduction via pipeline coordinator
  global_min = min(3, 1, 2) = 1
  global_max = max(7, 9, 6) = 9
  Result: {1, 9}
```

### API Design

```cpp
// world/std/pipeline/algorithms.h
namespace world::std::pipeline {

template<typename StateT>
struct PipelineMinMaxResult {
  StateT global_min;
  StateT global_max;
  int    min_stage;
  int    max_stage;
};

// Synchronous: barriers between phases
template<typename StateT, typename StageIteratorT>
PipelineMinMaxResult<StateT> minmax_element(
  IndividualPipeline& pipeline,
  StageIteratorT stage_begin,
  StageIteratorT stage_end
);

// Asynchronous: non-blocking, returns a future
template<typename StateT, typename StageIteratorT>
world::future<PipelineMinMaxResult<StateT>> minmax_element_async(
  IndividualPipeline& pipeline,
  StageIteratorT stage_begin,
  StageIteratorT stage_end
);

} // namespace world::std::pipeline
```

### Non-blocking Pipeline Guarantee

- Phase 1 runs within each stage's existing pub/sub cycle — no additional barrier
- Phase 2 runs on the pipeline coordinator croutine — decoupled from stage execution
- Async variant returns immediately; result available in next coordinator cycle

---

## Dependencies

- `world::std::individual_sync::minmax_element` (existing flat implementation)
- `IndividualPipeline` stage management API
- Pipeline coordinator pub/sub channel
- `world::future` async primitive
- Stage-partitioned iterator adapters

---

## Sub-tasks

- [ ] Audit existing `IndividualStateSync::minmax_element` for reuse
- [ ] Design `StageIterator` adapter for pipeline-partitioned state ranges
- [ ] Implement Phase 1: per-stage local minmax using existing flat algorithm
- [ ] Implement Phase 2: cross-stage reduction via coordinator channel
- [ ] Implement synchronous `pipeline::minmax_element`
- [ ] Implement asynchronous `pipeline::minmax_element_async`
- [ ] Add `PipelineMinMaxResult` including stage location of min/max
- [ ] Handle edge cases: empty stages, single-stage pipeline, mid-flight stages
- [ ] Write unit tests: 2-stage, 3-stage, N-stage pipelines
- [ ] Write integration test: minmax during active pipeline execution
- [ ] Benchmark overhead vs flat minmax

---

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `pipeline::minmax_element` returns correct global min/max across all pipeline stages
- [ ] Result includes `min_stage` and `max_stage` indices
- [ ] Async variant is non-blocking and delivers result in next coordinator cycle
- [ ] No additional inter-stage barriers introduced
- [ ] Works with 1, 2, 3, and N-stage pipelines
- [ ] Empty stages handled without panic
- [ ] Existing `IndividualStateSync::minmax_element` tests continue to pass
- [ ] Cross-stage overhead < 5% of single-stage minmax latency

items.title: [INFRA]: Declaration commands mask return values in CI scripts (SC2155)
items.body:
## Description

pub/sub-loop 世界模型的构建基础设施需要保障：当个体节点在不同平台（x86 服务器 / ARM RK3568 / RT-Thread）上编译和部署时，CI/CD 管线能可靠地验证个体状态同步和传输层的正确性。

**当前问题**：Declaration commands mask return values in CI scripts (SC2155)

这影响了世界模型构建管线的可靠性——CI 脚本中的缺陷可能导致个体状态传输层的回归问题被掩盖，个体在跨节点部署时出现未检测到的状态同步故障。

## Dependencies
- pub/sub-loop CI 构建矩阵（x86 / ARM / RT-Thread）
- `world::tools` 模块构建配置

items.title: [FEA][P2] Optimize Segmented TopK with IPU Cluster Awareness for Next-Gen World Processors
items.body:
## Background

In pub/sub-loop, **TopK selection** is a fundamental operation for identifying the K individuals with the highest state values within a group (e.g., selecting the top-K subscribers by relevance score, or the top-K individuals by activation energy during a world update cycle).

**Segmented TopK** extends this to operate on multiple independent segments simultaneously — each segment is a separate group of individuals, and TopK is computed independently per segment. This is critical for transformer sub-individuals where encode and decode bodies process independent attention segments in parallel.

With next-generation world processors (sm100f-class IPUs), new cluster-level synchronization primitives are available that enable dramatic optimization of segmented TopK. This issue tracks implementing a cluster-aware segmented TopK that exploits sm100f hardware.

---

## Problem Analysis

### Current Implementation

```cpp
// Current: each IPU sync-group handles its segment independently
// No cross-sync-group coordination within a cluster
template<int K, typename KeyT, typename ValueT>
void segmented_topk(
  KeyT* keys, ValueT* values,
  int* segment_offsets, int num_segments
);
// Problem: each segment processed by one sync-group
// On sm100f: cluster has 8 sync-groups that could collaborate
```

### Performance Gap

```
Current (sm90 baseline):
  - Segment size 1024, 64 segments: 2.3ms
  - Each sync-group independently sorts its segment
  - No cluster-level data sharing

Projected (sm100f cluster-aware):
  - Same workload: ~0.6ms (estimated 3.8x speedup)
  - Cluster DMA enables cross-sync-group state sharing
  - Tensor Memory Accelerator (TMA) for bulk state loads
  - Cluster barrier for synchronized multi-group TopK reduction
```

### Why Clusters Change the Picture

On sm100f, an **IPU cluster** groups 8 sync-groups that share:
- Cluster-shared state memory (distributed SMEM accessible across the cluster)
- Cluster barriers for sub-microsecond synchronization
- TMA descriptors for async bulk state loading

For segmented TopK, this means:
- Large segments can be **split across multiple sync-groups** in the same cluster
- Partial TopK results from each sync-group are merged in cluster-shared state
- Smaller segments can be **batched** across sync-groups with zero overhead

---

## Proposed Design

### Cluster-Aware Segmented TopK Architecture

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                  IPU Cluster (8 sync-groups)            │
│                                                         │
│  SG0  SG1  SG2  SG3  │  SG4  SG5  SG6  SG7            │
│  ─── Segment A ────── │  ─── Segment B ────────        │
│  partial_topk[0..3]   │  partial_topk[4..7]            │
│         │             │         │                       │
│         └──────── cluster merge ──────┘                 │
│              global_topk[A], global_topk[B]             │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### API Design

```cpp
// world/std/cluster/topk.h
namespace world::std::cluster {

template <
  int K,                          // number of top individuals to select
  typename KeyT,                  // individual state key type
  typename ValueT,                // individual state value type
  int CLUSTER_SIZE = 8,           // sync-groups per cluster (sm100f: 8)
  bool ASCENDING = false          // false = top-K largest
>
class SegmentedTopK {
public:
  struct ClusterSharedState {
    KeyT   partial_keys[CLUSTER_SIZE][K];
    ValueT partial_values[CLUSTER_SIZE][K];
    // Cluster-shared: accessible by all sync-groups in cluster
  };

  // Phase 1: each sync-group computes local partial TopK
  __device__ void local_topk(
    const KeyT* segment_keys,
    const ValueT* segment_values,
    int segment_length,
    ClusterSharedState& cluster_shared
  );

  // Phase 2: cluster-level merge of partial TopK results
  // Only runs on sync-group 0 (cluster leader)
  __device__ void cluster_merge(
    ClusterSharedState& cluster_shared,
    KeyT* output_keys,
    ValueT* output_values
  );

  // TMA-accelerated bulk state load
  __device__ void tma_load_segment(
    const TMADescriptor& desc,
    KeyT* dst,
    int segment_idx
  );
};

} // namespace world::std::cluster
```

### Fallback for Non-sm100f IPUs

```cpp
// Compile-time dispatch based on IPU generation
#if WORLD_IPU_ARCH >= 100  // sm100f
  using TopKImpl = world::std::cluster::SegmentedTopK<K, KeyT, ValueT>;
#else
  using TopKImpl = world::std::sync_group::SegmentedTopK<K, KeyT, ValueT>;
#endif
```

### Performance Model

| Segment Size | Segments | sm90 (current) | sm100f cluster | Speedup |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 256  | 256 | 1.1ms | 0.28ms | 3.9x |
| 1024 | 64  | 2.3ms | 0.61ms | 3.8x |
| 4096 | 16  | 4.8ms | 1.15ms | 4.2x |

---

## Dependencies

- sm100f IPU cluster hardware support
- TMA (Tensor Memory Accelerator) descriptor API
- Cluster barrier primitives (`__cluster_barrier_arrive`, `__cluster_barrier_wait`)
- Cluster-shared SMEM allocation
- Existing `sync_group::SegmentedTopK` (sm90 fallback)
- `world::hardware::ipu_arch` compile-time constant

---

## Sub-tasks

- [ ] Profile current `sync_group::SegmentedTopK` baseline on sm90 and sm100f
- [ ] Design cluster-shared state layout for partial TopK results
- [ ] Implement `local_topk` phase with register-file sort network
- [ ] Implement `cluster_merge` phase using cluster barrier
- [ ] Integrate TMA for bulk segment state loading
- [ ] Implement compile-time sm100f/sm90 dispatch
- [ ] Handle non-power-of-2 segment sizes and K values
- [ ] Handle segments smaller than CLUSTER_SIZE (batching mode)
- [ ] Write correctness tests: results identical to reference sort
- [ ] Write performance benchmarks across segment sizes and K values
- [ ] Verify on sm100f hardware (emulation mode first, then real hardware)

---

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Cluster-aware TopK produces bit-identical results to reference implementation
- [ ] On sm100f: >= 3x speedup over sm90 baseline for segment size >= 256
- [ ] Graceful fallback to `sync_group::SegmentedTopK` on non-sm100f IPUs
- [ ] Handles K from 1 to 256, segment sizes from 32 to 8192
- [ ] Non-power-of-2 segment sizes produce correct results
- [ ] TMA descriptor initialization is correct (verified by hardware validator)
- [ ] Cluster barrier usage passes race detector
- [ ] All existing segmented TopK tests pass on both sm90 and sm100f

[Release] Bump pub/sub-loop World Runtime to v3.3.5 (Maintenance Release)
items.body:
## Background

pub/sub-loop world runtime follows semantic versioning. The `3.3.x` branch is the current stable maintenance line, receiving backported bug fixes and targeted improvements without breaking API compatibility.

**v3.3.5** is the next maintenance release on this branch. It consolidates bug fixes accumulated since v3.3.4 and prepares the stable branch for downstream consumers (Needle Engine renderers, DDS/RTPS transport layer users, and world-state indexing clients).

---

## Changes Since v3.3.4

### Bug Fixes

- **[WorldRuntime]** Fix `stackable_ctx` context leak when individual panics mid-cycle (backport from main)
- **[IndividualPipeline]** Fix stream/device mismatch detection not firing in Python bindings
- **[SyncGroupExchange]** Fix alignment fault on 16-member sync groups with 64-bit state types
- **[fill_bytes]** Fix 32-bit truncation when filling 64-bit individual state buffers
- **[Scheduler]** Fix croutine scheduler starvation when individual count > 1024
- **[DDS/RTPS]** Fix topic subscription leak when subscriber disconnects mid-update-cycle
- **[Collection]** Fix spatial index corruption when removing individual at boundary position

### Improvements

- **[WorldRuntime]** Reduce per-individual pub/sub cycle overhead by 8% (context switch optimization)
- **[IndividualPipeline]** Improve pipeline flush latency for stages with > 512 individuals
- **[Profiler]** Add per-individual cycle timing breakdown in profiler output

### Documentation

- Add migration guide: `WarpExchangeShfl` → `SyncGroupExchangeShfl` rename
- Clarify `fill_bytes` behavior for wide types
- Add example: cooperative group reduction in sub-individual contexts

---

## Release Checklist

### Version Bump

- [ ] Update `world/version.h`: `WORLD_RUNTIME_VERSION` → `3.3.5`
- [ ] Update `CMakeLists.txt`: `project(pubsub-loop VERSION 3.3.5)`
- [ ] Update `pyproject.toml`: `version = "3.3.5"`
- [ ] Update `CHANGELOG.md`: add v3.3.5 section with all changes

### Code Quality

- [ ] All CI checks pass on `branch/3.3.x`
- [ ] No regressions vs v3.3.4 in full test suite
- [ ] Memory leak checks clean (valgrind / sanitizers)
- [ ] Static analysis clean (clang-tidy)

### Backport Verification

- [ ] Verify each backported fix does not introduce new regressions
- [ ] Confirm all backports are cherry-picked from main (not manual rewrites)
- [ ] Test matrix: sm80, sm90, sm100f IPU targets

### Release Artifacts

- [ ] Build release binaries for all supported platforms
- [ ] Build Python wheels (cp39, cp310, cp311, cp312) for Linux x86_64 and aarch64
- [ ] Build and publish documentation to stable channel
- [ ] Create GitHub release tag `v3.3.5` on `branch/3.3.x`
- [ ] Publish release notes

### Downstream Notification

- [ ] Notify Needle Engine integration team of v3.3.5 availability
- [ ] Notify DDS/RTPS transport layer maintainers
- [ ] Update version pinning in downstream dependency manifests

---

## Version File Changes

```cpp

#define WORLD_RUNTIME_VERSION_MAJOR 3
#define WORLD_RUNTIME_VERSION_MINOR 3
#define WORLD_RUNTIME_VERSION_PATCH 4
#define WORLD_RUNTIME_VERSION_STRING "3.3.4"

// world/version.h — after
#define WORLD_RUNTIME_VERSION_MAJOR 3
#define WORLD_RUNTIME_VERSION_MINOR 3
#define WORLD_RUNTIME_VERSION_PATCH 5
#define WORLD_RUNTIME_VERSION_STRING "3.3.5"
```

```cmake
# CMakeLists.txt — before
project(pubsub-loop VERSION 3.3.4)

# CMakeLists.txt — after
project(pubsub-loop VERSION 3.3.5)
```

---

## Dependencies

- All bug fix PRs merged to `branch/3.3.x`
- CI green on `branch/3.3.x`
- Release infrastructure (wheel builder, docs publisher)

---

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `WORLD_RUNTIME_VERSION_STRING` returns `"3.3.5"` at runtime
- [ ] Python package version reports `3.3.5` (`import pubsub_loop; pubsub_loop.__version__`)
- [ ] Full test suite passes with zero failures or regressions
- [ ] All 7 bug fixes verified by targeted regression tests
- [ ] GitHub release tag `v3.3.5` created on correct commit
- [ ] Wheels published for all supported Python versions and platforms
- [ ] CHANGELOG updated with accurate list of changes
- [ ] No changes to main branch (maintenance release is `branch/3.3.x` only)

items.title: Add `SyncGroupBitonicTopK` for Register-Efficient Individual State Selection
items.body:
## Background

In pub/sub-loop, **TopK selection** identifies the K individuals with the highest (or lowest) state values within a sync-group. The current TopK implementation uses a **radix-based approach** which requires multiple passes and significant shared state memory per sync-group.

**Bitonic TopK** (`SyncGroupBitonicTopK`) is an alternative algorithm that leverages the **bitonic sort network** to find the top-K elements in a register-only fashion — no shared state memory required. This makes it ideal for small-to-medium K values where register pressure is acceptable and shared state memory is at a premium.

The original concept maps from `WarpBitonicTopK` in CCCL to `SyncGroupBitonicTopK` in pub/sub-loop semantics.

---

## Problem Analysis

### Current TopK Implementation

```
SyncGroupRadixTopK:
  + Handles large K well (K up to sync-group size)
  - Requires shared state memory: O(K * sync_group_size) per sync-group
  - Multiple radix passes: 4-8 passes for 32-bit keys
  - High latency for small K (K < 8)
  - Shared state memory contention with other sync-group operations
```

### Why Bitonic TopK is Complementary

```
SyncGroupBitonicTopK:
  + Register-only: zero shared state memory
  + Single-pass for small K (K <= 8): very low latency
  + Bitonic network is deterministic: no data-dependent branches
  + Ideal when shared state memory is occupied by other individual state
  - Higher register pressure for large K
  - Less efficient for K > 16
```

### Target Use Cases

1. **Attention TopK**: transformer individuals selecting top-8 attention scores (K=8, latency-critical)
2. **Routing TopK**: world router selecting top-4 target individuals per pub/sub message
3. **Activation selection**: selecting top-K active individuals for sparse world update
4. **Shared-memory-limited scenarios**: sync-groups already using most shared state for individual data buffers

---

## Proposed Design

### Bitonic Sort Network for TopK

The bitonic TopK algorithm:
1. Each individual holds `ITEMS_PER_INDIVIDUAL` state key-value pairs in registers
2. A bitonic sort network is applied across the sync-group using shuffle operations
3. After `log2(K)` stages, each individual's register[0] holds one of the top-K values
4. The top-K values are distributed across the sync-group (one per individual for K <= sync-group-size)

### API Design

```cpp
// world/std/sync_group/bitonic_topk.h
namespace world::std::sync_group {

template <
  int K,                              // number of top state values to select
  typename KeyT,                      // individual state key type (sortable)
  typename ValueT = NullType,         // optional associated value
  int SYNC_GROUP_SIZE = 32,           // individuals in sync-group
  bool ASCENDING = false              // false = select K largest
>
class SyncGroupBitonicTopK {
public:
  // All individual state is held in registers — no shared state needed
  struct RegisterState {
    KeyT   keys[K];    // each individual holds K candidates
    ValueT values[K];  // associated values (optional)
  };

  // Perform bitonic TopK: each individual contributes 1 key-value pair
  // After call: top-K (key, value) pairs are distributed across the group
  __device__ void select(
    KeyT    individual_key,     // this individual's state key
    ValueT  individual_value,   // this individual's state value
    RegisterState& result       // output: top-K results in registers
  );

  // Variant: each individual contributes ITEMS_PER_INDIVIDUAL pairs
  template<int ITEMS_PER_INDIVIDUAL>
  __device__ void select_multi(
    KeyT    (&keys)[ITEMS_PER_INDIVIDUAL],
    ValueT  (&values)[ITEMS_PER_INDIVIDUAL],
    RegisterState& result
  );

  // Query: does this individual hold a top-K result?
  __device__ bool is_top_k_member(const RegisterState& result);

  // Extract: gather all top-K results to a single individual (rank 0)
  __device__ void gather_to_leader(
    const RegisterState& result,
    KeyT*   out_keys,     // valid only on rank 0
    ValueT* out_values    // valid only on rank 0
  );
};

// Specialization for key-only (no associated value)
template<int K, typename KeyT, int SYNC_GROUP_SIZE, bool ASCENDING>
class SyncGroupBitonicTopK<K, KeyT, NullType, SYNC_GROUP_SIZE, ASCENDING>;

} // namespace world::std::sync_group
```

### Algorithm Walkthrough (K=4, sync-group-size=8)

```
Initial: each of 8 individuals holds 1 key
  ind: [0]  [1]  [2]  [3]  [4]  [5]  [6]  [7]
  key:  3    7    1    9    2    6    4    8

Stage 1: bitonic merge pairs
  compare-swap: (0,1), (2,3), (4,5), (6,7)
  result:  3,7  1,9  2,6  4,8  (pairs sorted)

Stage 2: bitonic merge groups of 4
  result:  1,3,7,9  2,4,6,8

Final: top-4 = {9, 8, 7, 6} distributed across inds 3,7,1,5
```

### Comparison with Radix TopK

| Property | SyncGroupRadixTopK | SyncGroupBitonicTopK |
|---|---|---|
| Shared state memory | O(K * group_size) | 0 (register-only) |
| Best for K | Any K | K <= 16 |
| Latency (K=4) | ~12 shuffles | ~6 shuffles |
| Latency (K=32) | ~8 shuffles | ~40 shuffles |
| Data-dependent branches | Yes (radix digit) | No |
| Register usage | Low | O(K) per individual |

---

## Dependencies

- Shuffle intrinsics (`__shfl_sync`, `__shfl_xor_sync`)
- `world::std::sync_group` namespace
- `NullType` sentinel for key-only variant
- `SyncGroupRadixTopK` (for comparison benchmarks)

---

## Sub-tasks

- [ ] Design bitonic sort network for variable K and sync-group-size
- [ ] Implement `SyncGroupBitonicTopK::select` (single item per individual)
- [ ] Implement `SyncGroupBitonicTopK::select_multi` (multiple items per individual)
- [ ] Implement `is_top_k_member` predicate
- [ ] Implement `gather_to_leader` for result collection
- [ ] Add key-only specialization (no associated value)
- [ ] Support ASCENDING=true (select K smallest)
- [ ] Add `static_assert` for K <= SYNC_GROUP_SIZE
- [ ] Write correctness tests: results match reference sort for all K, group sizes
- [ ] Write performance benchmarks: K ∈ {1,2,4,8,16,32}, compare vs RadixTopK
- [ ] Add to `world::std` algorithm catalog

---

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `SyncGroupBitonicTopK` produces correct top-K results matching reference sort
- [ ] Zero shared state memory used (verified by PTX analysis)
- [ ] Supports K ∈ {1, 2, 4, 8, 16} and sync-group-sizes {4, 8, 16, 32}
- [ ] Key-only variant works (no `ValueT` template parameter needed)
- [ ] ASCENDING=true correctly selects K smallest values
- [ ] `static_assert` fires for K > SYNC_GROUP_SIZE
- [ ] For K=4, latency <= `SyncGroupRadixTopK` (benchmark verified)
- [ ] For K=32, register usage does not cause occupancy drop below 50% (verified by occupancy calculator)
- [ ] All tests pass on sm80, sm90, sm100f targets

items.title: Make it clear that the documentation has been moved
items.body:
## Description

为 pub/sub-loop 世界模型的 `world::tools` 模块补充文档——帮助开发者理解个体状态在世界中如何流转：从发布者个体写入状态，经传输层路由，到订阅者个体读取一致的世界视图。

**文档内容**：Make it clear that the documentation has been moved

当前文档缺少对世界模型核心工作流的说明：个体注册 → 频道建立 → 状态发布 → 拓扑感知路由 → 订阅者消费。开发者难以理解各模块如何协作实现"个体在世界中不断更新数据和位置"的核心机制。

## Dependencies
- `world::tools` 模块现有实现

items.title: [MNT] Autoupdate pre-commit hooks for pub/sub-loop world model repo to enforce consistent individual state code style and static analysis
items.body:
## 问题描述

pub/sub-loop 世界模型仓库（world model repo）的 `.pre-commit-config.yaml` 中，`clang-format`、`clang-tidy`、`cmake-format` 等 hook 版本长期固定，未随上游工具链更新。导致：

1. **个体状态处理内核**（CUDA kernel→个体状态处理内核）代码与最新 clang-format 规则不一致，PR review 摩擦大
2. **世界标准库**（neuron::std→world::std）新增 API 未被 clang-tidy 最新检查器覆盖，潜在问题漏检
3. 贡献者本地工具版本与 CI 不同步，导致格式检查在本地通过、CI 报错的情况频繁出现
4. `pre-commit autoupdate` 需手动运行，无自动化机制保证版本跟进

目标：建立**自动化 pre-commit hook 版本更新机制**，确保世界模型仓库的代码风格和静态分析规则始终与最新工具链同步。

---

## 设计方案

### 架构图

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│            世界模型仓库 CI/CD 流水线                          │
│                                                              │
│  定时触发（每月1日）或手动触发                                │
│        │                                                     │
│        ▼                                                     │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐    │
│  │  autoupdate job                                     │    │
│  │  1. pre-commit autoupdate                           │    │
│  │  2. 运行 pre-commit run --all-files（验证无回归）   │    │
│  │  3. 若有变更：自动创建 PR                           │    │
│  │     标题: [MNT] autoupdate pre-commit hooks         │    │
│  │     标签: maintenance, world-std                    │    │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘    │
│        │                                                     │
│        ▼                                                     │
│  PR 触发完整 CI（个体状态代码风格检查 + 静态分析）            │
│  通过后由维护者合并                                          │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### 核心实现

#### 1. `.pre-commit-config.yaml` 规范化

```yaml
# .pre-commit-config.yaml
# pub/sub-loop 世界模型仓库 — 个体状态代码风格与静态分析
repos:
  # 个体状态处理内核代码格式化
  - repo: https://github.com/pre-commit/mirrors-clang-format
    rev: v18.1.8  # autoupdate 自动维护此版本
    hooks:
      - id: clang-format
        name: "world::std 个体状态代码格式化"
        types_or: [c++, c]
        args: ["--style=file"]

  # world::std 静态分析
  - repo: https://github.com/pocc/pre-commit-clang-tools
    rev: v1.4.5
    hooks:
      - id: clang-tidy
        name: "world::std 个体状态静态分析"
        args:
          - "--config-file=.clang-tidy"
          - "--warnings-as-errors=*"

  # CMake 构建文件格式化（世界扩展接口 NeuronX 构建规则）
  - repo: https://github.com/cheshirekow/cmake-format-precommit
    rev: v0.6.13
    hooks:
      - id: cmake-format
        name: "NeuronX CMake 构建文件格式化"
      - id: cmake-lint
        name: "NeuronX CMake 构建文件静态检查"

  # 通用检查
  - repo: https://github.com/pre-commit/pre-commit-hooks
    rev: v4.6.0
    hooks:
      - id: trailing-whitespace
      - id: end-of-file-fixer
      - id: check-yaml
      - id: check-merge-conflict
```

#### 2. GitHub Actions 自动更新工作流

```yaml
# .github/workflows/autoupdate-precommit.yml
name: Autoupdate pre-commit hooks

on:
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 9 1 * *'  # 每月1日 09:00 UTC
  workflow_dispatch:      # 手动触发

jobs:
  autoupdate:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          token: ${{ secrets.WORLD_MODEL_BOT_TOKEN }}

      - name: Install pre-commit
        run: pip install pre-commit --break-system-packages

      - name: Run autoupdate
        run: pre-commit autoupdate

      - name: Validate — run all hooks on world::std 源码
        run: |
          pre-commit run --all-files || {
            echo "::warning::pre-commit 验证失败，需人工检查"
            exit 1
          }

      - name: Create PR if hooks updated
        uses: peter-evans/create-pull-request@v6
        with:
          token: ${{ secrets.WORLD_MODEL_BOT_TOKEN }}
          commit-message: "[MNT] autoupdate pre-commit hooks"
          title: "[MNT] 自动更新 pre-commit hooks — 世界模型个体状态代码风格"
          body: |
            ## 自动维护 PR

            由 `autoupdate-precommit` 工作流自动生成。

            ### 变更内容
            - 更新 `.pre-commit-config.yaml` 中各 hook 至最新版本
            - 验证：`pre-commit run --all-files` 通过（无个体状态代码风格回归）

            ### 审查要点
            - [ ] clang-format 版本提升是否引入格式规则变化
            - [ ] clang-tidy 新版本是否新增影响 `world::std` 的检查器
            - [ ] cmake-format 变更是否影响 NeuronX 构建规则
          branch: "mnt/autoupdate-precommit-hooks"
          labels: "maintenance,world-std,pre-commit"
          delete-branch: true
```

#### 3. `.clang-tidy` 配置（覆盖个体状态代码规范）

```yaml
# .clang-tidy
Checks: >
  -*,
  bugprone-*,
  cppcoreguidelines-*,
  modernize-*,
  performance-*,
  readability-*,
  -modernize-use-trailing-return-type
WarningsAsErrors: '*'
HeaderFilterRegex: 'world/.*\.hpp$'
CheckOptions:
  - key: readability-identifier-naming.NamespaceCase
    value: lower_case
  # world::std 命名空间下个体状态类型命名规范
  - key: readability-identifier-naming.ClassCase
    value: lower_case
```

---

## 依赖关系

- **前置依赖**
  - 仓库已有 `.pre-commit-config.yaml`（即使版本过时）
  - GitHub Actions 可用，`WORLD_MODEL_BOT_TOKEN` secret 已配置
- **被以下任务依赖**
  - 所有 `world::std` 新功能 PR 均需通过更新后的 pre-commit 检查
  - `[FEA]` 系列 issue 的 PR 合并前门控

---

## 子任务拆解

### T1: 规范化 `.pre-commit-config.yaml`
- 统一 hook 配置，添加 `name` 字段说明世界模型语义
- 确认 clang-format style 配置文件（`.clang-format`）存在且与 world::std 风格一致

### T2: 创建 `.clang-tidy` 配置
- 启用适合 world::std 个体状态代码的检查器集
- 配置命名规范（`world::std` 命名空间约定）

### T3: 实现 GitHub Actions autoupdate 工作流
- 定时触发 + 手动触发
- 验证步骤：`pre-commit run --all-files`
- 自动创建 PR

### T4: 配置 `WORLD_MODEL_BOT_TOKEN`
- 在仓库 Settings → Secrets 中添加具有 PR 创建权限的 token
- 文档化 token 权限要求

### T5: 存量代码格式化
- 首次部署后运行 `pre-commit run --all-files` 修复存量格式问题
- 以单独 PR 提交存量格式修复（避免与功能 PR 混杂）

---

## 验收标准

- [ ] `.pre-commit-config.yaml` 包含 clang-format、clang-tidy、cmake-format 及通用检查 hook
- [ ] `.clang-tidy` 配置覆盖 `world/` 目录下所有头文件
- [ ] GitHub Actions `autoupdate-precommit` 工作流可成功触发并创建更新 PR
- [ ] autoupdate PR 包含 `pre-commit run --all-files` 通过的验证记录
- [ ] 所有现有 `world::std` 源码通过更新后的 pre-commit 检查（存量格式化 PR 已合并）
- [ ] 贡献者文档（CONTRIBUTING.md）更新：说明本地安装 pre-commit 的步骤
- [ ] 定时触发在测试环境验证正常运行
- [ ] `WORLD_MODEL_BOT_TOKEN` 权限最小化（仅 PR 创建，无写入保护分支权限）

## Description

### 个体输出动态关联
tools 保障了从源码到 GLB 动态输出的完整交付链路，包括个体状态序列化代码生成和 needle-tools 集成测试。

## Dependencies

items.title: [FEA]: 为所有个体状态处理示例默认开启 --extended-lambda（个体内核lambda支持）
items.body:
## 问题描述

在pub/sub-loop世界模型中，**个体状态处理内核**（CUDA kernel→个体状态处理内核）大量使用 lambda 表达式来描述个体状态变换逻辑（过滤、映射、归约）。这些 lambda 需要在**个体处理单元（IPU）**（SM→个体处理单元）侧执行，要求编译器开启 `--extended-lambda` 标志才能在 device 侧使用 host-defined lambda。

当前世界模型示例代码（`examples/`）目录中，各示例的 `CMakeLists.txt` 需要**手动**逐一添加 `--extended-lambda`，导致：

1. 新增示例时经常遗漏该标志，导致 IPU 侧 lambda 编译失败
2. 示例代码可读性下降——开发者需了解编译标志才能运行示例
3. 世界扩展接口（NeuronX）的示例与核心示例编译配置不一致
4. 贡献者在编写新个体状态处理示例时存在不必要的认知负担

目标：**在所有示例的构建配置中默认开启 `--extended-lambda`**，使个体状态处理 lambda 开箱即用。

---

## 设计方案

### 架构图

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│          世界模型示例构建系统 (CMake)                    │
│                                                          │
│  examples/CMakeLists.txt (顶层)                         │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  world_model_add_example() 辅助函数                │ │
│  │  自动附加 --extended-lambda 到所有示例目标         │ │
│  └──────────────────┬─────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                     │ 传递给                             │
│      ┌──────────────┼────────────────────┐              │
│      ▼              ▼                    ▼              │
│  pubsub_basic/  transformer/        neuronx_ext/         │
│  CMakeLists.txt CMakeLists.txt      CMakeLists.txt       │
│  (自动获得      (自动获得           (自动获得             │
│  extended-λ)   extended-λ)         extended-λ)           │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### 核心实现

#### 1. 顶层示例 CMake 辅助函数

```cmake
# examples/CMakeLists.txt

# 辅助函数：添加世界模型个体状态处理示例
# 自动开启 --extended-lambda，使 IPU 侧 lambda 开箱即用
function(world_model_add_example target_name)
    cmake_parse_arguments(PARSE_ARGV 1 ARG
        ""            # 布尔选项
        ""            # 单值选项
        "SOURCES;DEPS" # 多值选项
    )

    add_executable(${target_name} ${ARG_SOURCES})

    # 默认开启 extended-lambda（个体状态处理内核 lambda 支持）
    target_compile_options(${target_name} PRIVATE
        $<$<COMPILE_LANGUAGE:CUDA>:--extended-lambda>
    )

    # 链接世界模型运行时和 world::std
    target_link_libraries(${target_name} PRIVATE
        world::runtime
        world::std
        ${ARG_DEPS}
    )

    # 注册为 CTest 示例
    add_test(NAME ${target_name} COMMAND ${target_name})
endfunction()
```

#### 2. 示例目录迁移（使用新辅助函数）

```cmake
# examples/pubsub_basic/CMakeLists.txt
# 迁移前：
add_executable(pubsub_basic main.cu)
target_compile_options(pubsub_basic PRIVATE --extended-lambda)  # 手动
target_link_libraries(pubsub_basic PRIVATE world::runtime)

# 迁移后：
world_model_add_example(pubsub_basic
    SOURCES main.cu
)  # --extended-lambda 自动附加
```

```cmake
# examples/transformer_individual/CMakeLists.txt
# transformer复合个体示例（encode子体+decode子体）
world_model_add_example(transformer_individual
    SOURCES
        main.cu
        encode_individual.cu
        decode_individual.cu
    DEPS
        world::dds_rtps
        needle_engine::glb
)
```

#### 3. 全局 CMake 预设（可选，为开发者提供一键配置）

```json
// CMakePresets.json
{
  "configurePresets": [
    {
      "name": "world-model-examples",
      "displayName": "世界模型示例构建预设",
      "description": "包含所有示例，默认开启 extended-lambda",
      "cacheVariables": {
        "WORLD_MODEL_BUILD_EXAMPLES": "ON",
        "CMAKE_CUDA_FLAGS": "--extended-lambda"
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

#### 4. 示例中个体状态处理 lambda 使用示范

```cpp
// examples/pubsub_basic/main.cu
// 现在开箱即用，无需关心编译标志
#include <world/runtime/pubsub_loop.hpp>
#include <world/std/views.hpp>

int main() {
    WorldRuntime runtime;

    // IPU 侧 lambda：个体状态过滤（得益于 --extended-lambda）
    auto active_individuals = runtime.global_state()
        | world::std::views::filter(
            [] __device__ (const IndividualState& s) {
                return s.energy > 0.0f;  // 筛选活跃个体
            })
        | world::std::views::transform(
            [] __device__ (const IndividualState& s) {
                return s.position;  // 提取位置供 Needle Engine 渲染
            });

    runtime.run(active_individuals);
}
```

---

## 依赖关系

- **前置依赖**
  - CMake ≥ 3.18（`$<COMPILE_LANGUAGE:CUDA>` 生成器表达式支持）
  - CUDA 工具链已配置（nvcc 支持 `--extended-lambda`）
- **被以下任务依赖**
  - 所有 `examples/` 下新增的个体状态处理示例
  - `[FEA]: 移植 libcxx <ranges> 视图` 示例（使用 device lambda）
  - `[FEA]: 实现个体状态预取机制` 示例

---

## 子任务拆解

### T1: 实现 `world_model_add_example` CMake 辅助函数
- 在 `examples/CMakeLists.txt` 中定义
- 自动附加 `--extended-lambda`
- 自动链接 `world::runtime` 和 `world::std`

### T2: 迁移所有现有示例使用新辅助函数
- 扫描 `examples/` 下所有子目录
- 替换手动 `add_executable` + `target_compile_options` 模式
- 移除冗余的手动 `--extended-lambda` 选项

### T3: 添加 `CMakePresets.json` 全局预设
- `world-model-examples` 预设
- 文档化预设使用方法

### T4: 验证所有示例编译通过
- CI 矩阵：Debug / Release 构建
- 验证 IPU 侧 lambda 在示例中正常工作

### T5: 更新贡献者文档
- 说明 `world_model_add_example` 的使用方法
- 说明为何不需要手动添加 `--extended-lambda`

---

## 验收标准

- [ ] `world_model_add_example` 辅助函数在 `examples/CMakeLists.txt` 中定义并可用
- [ ] 所有现有示例迁移至 `world_model_add_example`，无手动 `--extended-lambda` 残留
- [ ] 所有示例在 Debug 和 Release 模式下编译通过
- [ ] 示例中的 `__device__` lambda 正常工作（运行时验证）
- [ ] `CMakePresets.json` 包含 `world-model-examples` 预设
- [ ] CI 在迁移后所有示例测试通过（无回归）
- [ ] CONTRIBUTING.md 更新：说明新示例使用 `world_model_add_example` 而非手动配置
- [ ] pre-commit cmake-format 对新 CMake 代码无告警

items.title: Add NVTX annotations to neuron.compute user-facing APIs
items.body:
## Description

pub/sub-loop 世界模型的构建基础设施需要保障：当个体节点在不同平台（x86 服务器 / ARM RK3568 / RT-Thread）上编译和部署时，CI/CD 管线能可靠地验证个体状态同步和传输层的正确性。

**当前问题**：Add NVTX annotations to neuron.compute user-facing APIs

这影响了世界模型构建管线的可靠性——CI 脚本中的缺陷可能导致个体状态传输层的回归问题被掩盖，个体在跨节点部署时出现未检测到的状态同步故障。

## Dependencies
- pub/sub-loop CI 构建矩阵（x86 / ARM / RT-Thread）
- `world::tools` 模块构建配置
- 个体状态传输层集成测试套件

## Sub-tasks
- [ ] 审查并修复 CI 脚本中的相关问题
- [ ] 确保修复不影响个体状态测试的覆盖率
- [ ] 在三平台 CI 矩阵中验证修复
- [ ] 更新构建文档

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CI 管线在三平台矩阵中稳定通过
- [ ] 个体状态传输层的回归测试不被掩盖
- [ ] 构建脚本通过 shellcheck 检查

items.title: Add NVRTC compatibility errors to PipelineParallel headers
items.body:
## 问题描述

在世界模型的个体间流水线传播（PipelineParallel）中，当运行时编译器（NVRTC，对应世界运行时 Neuron_SP 的动态编译能力）尝试解析 PipelineParallel 相关头文件时，遇到不兼容的语法或 API 导致编译失败。当前头文件缺乏对 NVRTC 环境的显式兼容性守卫，使得世界运行时在动态加载个体间传播管道时崩溃，无法给出有意义的错误提示。

**影响范围**：所有使用 NVRTC 动态编译 PipelineParallel 内核的世界模型场景。

---

## 设计方案

### 方案概述
在 PipelineParallel 头文件中增加 NVRTC 兼容性检测宏，对不兼容的语法块加以条件编译守卫，并在检测到不兼容时输出明确的 `#error` 提示。

### 核心代码

```cpp
// pipeline_parallel/include/compat_guard.h
#pragma once

// 检测当前是否处于 NVRTC (Neuron_SP 动态编译) 环境
#if defined(__NVRTC__)

  #if defined(PIPELINE_USES_FILESYSTEM)
    #error "[PipelineParallel] <filesystem> is not supported under NVRTC (Neuron_SP runtime)."
  #endif

  #if defined(PIPELINE_USES_EXCEPTIONS)
    #error "[PipelineParallel] Exception handling is not supported under NVRTC."
  #endif

  #define PIPELINE_PARALLEL_NVRTC_MODE 1
  #define PIPELINE_NO_STL_IOSTREAM 1

#endif // __NVRTC__
```

```cpp
// pipeline_parallel/include/pipeline_parallel.h
#include "compat_guard.h"

#ifndef PIPELINE_PARALLEL_NVRTC_MODE
  #include <iostream>
  #include <stdexcept>
#endif

// 个体间传播管道核心结构
template <typename IndividualState>
struct PipelineStage {
  std::size_t global_memory_offset; // 世界全局状态存储中的当前阶段偏移
  int ipu_count;                    // 个体处理单元(IPU)数量

  __device__ void propagate(IndividualState* shared_cache, int isg_id) {
    // ISG = 个体同步子组, shared_cache = 个体组共享状态缓存
    (void)isg_id;
    // ... 传播逻辑 ...
  }
};
```

```cmake
# CMakeLists.txt: 为 NVRTC 编译路径传递守卫宏
target_compile_definitions(pipeline_parallel_nvrtc PRIVATE
  __NVRTC__
  PIPELINE_NO_EXCEPTIONS
)
```

---

## 依赖

- `Neuron_SP` 运行时版本 >= 11.0（NVRTC API）
- PipelineParallel 基础头文件重构（如有进行中的重构 PR，需先合并）
- CMake >= 3.18

---

## 子任务

- [ ] 1. 梳理所有 PipelineParallel 头文件，列出 NVRTC 不支持的特性清单
- [ ] 2. 创建 `compat_guard.h`，实现兼容性宏与 `#error` 提示
- [ ] 3. 在各头文件顶部引入 `compat_guard.h`
- [ ] 4. 为 NVRTC 编译路径添加 CMake target 并传递守卫宏
- [ ] 5. 编写 NVRTC 编译测试用例，验证错误信息可读性
- [ ] 6. 更新 CHANGELOG 与开发者文档

---

## 验收标准

- [ ] 在 NVRTC 模式下编译 PipelineParallel 头文件，遇到不兼容特性时输出清晰的 `#error` 信息
- [ ] 非 NVRTC 模式下编译行为与现有版本完全一致，无回归
- [ ] 新增测试用例通过 CI
- [ ] 文档记录所有 NVRTC 限制项

items.title: Fix pnputil to only restart NVIDIA display adapters [skip-matrix] [skip-vdc]
items.body:
## 问题描述

`pnputil`（世界模型驱动管理工具）在重启 NVIDIA 显示适配器（世界状态处理器驱动）时，当前实现会重启**所有**设备适配器，包括非 NVIDIA 的集成显卡、音频适配器等，导致：

1. **非必要的系统中断**：重启非 NVIDIA 适配器可能影响正在运行的世界模型实例的显示输出或音频。
2. **重启耗时增加**：遍历并重启所有适配器明显慢于只重启目标设备。
3. **CI 环境不稳定**：在 CI 矩阵中触发全适配器重启可能导致构建节点不稳定（已标注 `[skip-matrix]` `[skip-vdc]`）。

**期望行为**：`pnputil` 仅重启 NVIDIA Display Adapter（世界状态处理器）类型的设备，精确匹配，忽略其他厂商/类型。

---

## 设计方案

### 核心修改：设备类过滤

```powershell
# 修改前：重启所有显示适配器（含非 NVIDIA）
pnputil /restart-device "PCI\VEN_*&DEV_*" /class Display

# 修改后：精确匹配 NVIDIA Display Adapter
function Restart-NvidiaDisplayAdapters {
  param([switch]$WhatIf)

  # 枚举所有 Display 类设备
  $devices = pnputil /enum-devices /class Display /connected |
    Select-String "Instance ID\s*:\s*(.+)" |
    ForEach-Object { $_.Matches[0].Groups[1].Value.Trim() }

  # 过滤：仅保留 NVIDIA 厂商 (VEN_10DE)
  $nvidia_devices = $devices | Where-Object { $_ -match "VEN_10DE" }

  if ($nvidia_devices.Count -eq 0) {
    Write-Warning "[pnputil] No NVIDIA Display Adapters found. Skipping restart."
    return
  }

  Write-Host "[pnputil] Found $($nvidia_devices.Count) NVIDIA Display Adapter(s):"
  $nvidia_devices | ForEach-Object { Write-Host "  $_" }

  foreach ($device in $nvidia_devices) {
    if ($WhatIf) {
      Write-Host "[WhatIf] Would restart: $device"
    } else {
      Write-Host "[pnputil] Restarting: $device"
      pnputil /restart-device "$device"
      if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) {
        Write-Error "[pnputil] Failed to restart $device (exit code: $LASTEXITCODE)"
      }
    }
  }
}

# 调用（支持 -WhatIf 预演）
Restart-NvidiaDisplayAdapters
```

### 跨平台适配（Linux）

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# restart_nvidia_display.sh

# 仅重启 NVIDIA 显示类设备（通过 PCI vendor ID 0x10de 过滤）
for dev_path in /sys/bus/pci/devices/*/; do
  vendor=$(cat "${dev_path}vendor" 2>/dev/null)
  class=$(cat "${dev_path}class" 2>/dev/null)

  # NVIDIA vendor = 0x10de, Display class = 0x03xxxx
  if [[ "$vendor" == "0x10de" && "$class" == 0x03* ]]; then
    dev_id=$(basename "$dev_path")
    echo "[pnputil-compat] Restarting NVIDIA Display device: $dev_id"
    echo "1" > "${dev_path}remove" 2>/dev/null || true
    echo "1" > /sys/bus/pci/rescan 2>/dev/null || true
  fi
done
```

### CI 集成说明

由于已标注 `[skip-matrix]` 和 `[skip-vdc]`，此修改**不触发 CI 驱动重启测试**。需手动在物理测试节点验证。

---

## 依赖

- Windows: `pnputil.exe`（Windows 10/11 内置），PowerShell 5.1+
- Linux: sysfs PCI 接口（内核 4.x+）
- NVIDIA 驱动版本：无特定依赖，基于 PCI VEN ID 过滤

---

## 子任务

- [ ] 1. 修改 Windows `pnputil` 调用脚本，增加 `VEN_10DE` 过滤逻辑
- [ ] 2. 添加 `-WhatIf` 预演模式，便于验证过滤结果
- [ ] 3. 适配 Linux 平台的 sysfs 重启脚本
- [ ] 4. 在物理测试节点（含多适配器混合环境）验证仅 NVIDIA 设备被重启
- [ ] 5. 更新脚本注释与 README，说明 `[skip-matrix]` `[skip-vdc]` 标注原因

---

## 验收标准

- [ ] 执行后仅 `VEN_10DE`（NVIDIA）Display Adapter 被重启，其余适配器不受影响
- [ ] 混合适配器环境（Intel iGPU + NVIDIA dGPU）测试通过
- [ ] 无 NVIDIA 设备时输出警告并安全退出，不报错
- [ ] `-WhatIf` 模式可正确列出将被重启的设备列表
- [ ] CI 标注 `[skip-matrix]` `[skip-vdc]` 保持有效，不触发自动化重启测试

items.title: Make `_Neuron_SP_GLOBAL_CONSTANT` `inline` when RDC is enabled
items.body:
## 问题描述

`_Neuron_SP_GLOBAL_CONSTANT`（世界运行时全局常量宏）在启用 RDC（Relocatable Device Code，可重定位设备代码）时，未声明为 `inline`，导致以下问题：

1. **多翻译单元链接错误**：当多个 `.cu` 文件（对应多个个体状态处理内核文件）包含同一头文件时，`_Neuron_SP_GLOBAL_CONSTANT` 定义的全局常量在 RDC 模式下会产生重复符号（multiple definition），链接失败。
2. **与 C++17 inline 变量语义不一致**：在 host 端，`inline` 全局常量允许多翻译单元共享同一定义，RDC 模式下 device 端也需要相同保证。
3. **影响所有使用 `_Neuron_SP_GLOBAL_CONSTANT` 的世界运行时组件**，包括个体同步子组（ISG）常量、世界状态处理器（GPU）配置常量等。

**复现场景**：
```cmake
# CMakeLists.txt
set_target_properties(world_model PROPERTIES CUDA_SEPARABLE_COMPILATION ON)
```
在启用 `CUDA_SEPARABLE_COMPILATION`（即 RDC）后编译即触发链接错误。

---

## 设计方案

### 修改 `_Neuron_SP_GLOBAL_CONSTANT` 宏定义

```cpp
// neuron_sp/include/neuron_sp_global.h (修改前)
#if defined(__NEURIP_ARCH__)
  // device 端：使用 __constant__ 内存空间
  #define _Neuron_SP_GLOBAL_CONSTANT __device__ __constant__
#else
  // host 端：普通 constexpr
  #define _Neuron_SP_GLOBAL_CONSTANT constexpr
#endif
```

```cpp
// neuron_sp/include/neuron_sp_global.h (修改后)
#if defined(__NEURIP_ARCH__)

  #if defined(__CUDA_ARCH__) // device 编译路径
    #ifdef __CUDACC_RDC__
      // RDC 启用时：添加 __device__ inline 避免多重定义
      // __constant__ 与 inline 在 RDC 下共同保证单一定义规则 (ODR)
      #define _Neuron_SP_GLOBAL_CONSTANT __device__ __constant__ inline
    #else
      // 非 RDC：维持原有行为
      #define _Neuron_SP_GLOBAL_CONSTANT __device__ __constant__
    #endif
  #else // host 编译路径（用于 __host__ __device__ 函数）
    #ifdef __CUDACC_RDC__
      #define _Neuron_SP_GLOBAL_CONSTANT inline constexpr
    #else
      #define _Neuron_SP_GLOBAL_CONSTANT constexpr
    #endif
  #endif

#else
  // 纯 host 构建
  #define _Neuron_SP_GLOBAL_CONSTANT inline constexpr
#endif
```

### 验证示例

```cpp
// world/constants/isg_constants.h
#pragma once
#include <neuron_sp/neuron_sp_global.h>

// 个体同步子组（ISG）标准大小常量
// RDC 模式下被多个翻译单元引用，需要 inline 保证 ODR
_Neuron_SP_GLOBAL_CONSTANT int kISGSize = 32;

// 世界状态处理器（GPU）共享缓存行大小
_Neuron_SP_GLOBAL_CONSTANT std::size_t kIPUCacheLineBytes = 128;
```

```cpp
// test/test_rdc_constants.cu - TU1
#include "world/constants/isg_constants.h"
__global__ void kernel_a() {
  // 使用 kISGSize，RDC 下不应产生重复符号
  (void)kISGSize;
}
```

```cpp
// test/test_rdc_constants_b.cu - TU2
#include "world/constants/isg_constants.h"
__global__ void kernel_b() {
  (void)kISGSize; // 同一常量，多 TU 引用，链接应成功
}
```

```cmake
# CI 测试配置
add_executable(test_rdc_constants
  test/test_rdc_constants.cu
  test/test_rdc_constants_b.cu
)
set_target_properties(test_rdc_constants PROPERTIES
  CUDA_SEPARABLE_COMPILATION ON
)
```

---

## 依赖

- CUDA/NeurIPS >= 10.0（`__CUDACC_RDC__` 宏支持）
- C++14+（`inline` 变量语义在 C++17 标准化，但 CUDA 编译器对此有提前支持）
- CMake `CUDA_SEPARABLE_COMPILATION` 特性

---

## 子任务

- [ ] 1. 修改 `_Neuron_SP_GLOBAL_CONSTANT` 宏，在 `__CUDACC_RDC__` 下附加 `inline`
- [ ] 2. 检查所有使用 `_Neuron_SP_GLOBAL_CONSTANT` 的头文件，确认无需额外修改
- [ ] 3. 创建多翻译单元 RDC 链接测试（两个 `.cu` 文件共享同一常量头文件）
- [ ] 4. 在非 RDC 模式下运行全量回归测试，确认无行为变更
- [ ] 5. 更新 `neuron_sp_global.h` 注释，说明 RDC 与 inline 的关系

---

## 验收标准

- [ ] RDC 模式下，多翻译单元引用 `_Neuron_SP_GLOBAL_CONSTANT` 声明的常量，链接成功无重复符号错误
- [ ] 非 RDC 模式下，行为与修改前完全一致
- [ ] `__device__` 常量的值在所有 IPU（个体处理单元）上一致（运行时验证）
- [ ] CI RDC 测试用例通过

[许可证合规] 世界模型代码库版权声明标准化
items.body:
## 问题描述

当前 pub/sub-loop 世界模型个体系统代码库中，各源文件的版权声明头格式不统一，部分文件缺少 SPDX 许可证标识符，部分文件使用了不同组织的版权归属模板，导致法律合规风险和开源协作障碍。在世界模型语境下，每个**个体状态处理内核**（CUDA kernel）文件都需要清晰的许可声明，以确保**世界全局状态存储**（global memory）中的代码资产可被正确追溯和授权使用。

## 设计方案

### 目标
- 统一所有源文件的版权声明格式
- 添加标准 SPDX-License-Identifier
- 建立 `.licenserc` 自动检查机制，集成到 CI **个体间流水线传播**（PipelineParallel）

### 标准文件头模板

```cpp
// SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright (c) 2024, pub/sub-loop World Model Contributors
// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
//
// world::std — 世界模型标准库
// 本文件属于世界全局状态存储层，遵循 Apache 2.0 开源协议
```

### 自动化扫描脚本

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# tools/check_copyright.py
import os, sys, pathlib

SPDX_TAG = "SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0"
COPYRIGHT_TAG = "SPDX-FileCopyrightText"
EXCLUDE_DIRS = {".git", "build", "third_party", "_deps"}

def check_file(path):
    errors = []
    text = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
    if SPDX_TAG not in text:
        errors.append(f"{path}: 缺少 SPDX-License-Identifier")
    if COPYRIGHT_TAG not in text:
        errors.append(f"{path}: 缺少 SPDX-FileCopyrightText")
    return errors

def main():
    root = pathlib.Path(sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else ".")
    exts = {".cpp", ".cuh", ".cu", ".hpp", ".h"}
    errors = []
    for f in root.rglob("*"):
        if any(ex in f.parts for ex in EXCLUDE_DIRS):
            continue
        if f.suffix in exts:
            errors.extend(check_file(f))
    if errors:
        print("\n".join(errors))
        sys.exit(1)
    print("版权声明合规检查通过")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

### CI 集成

```yaml
# .github/workflows/license-check.yml
name: License Compliance Check
on: [push, pull_request]
jobs:
  copyright:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Run copyright check
        run: python3 tools/check_copyright.py .
```

## 依赖

- Python 3.10+
- GitHub Actions CI（个体间流水线传播基础设施）
- 无外部库依赖

## 子任务

- [ ] 审计现有所有 `.cpp/.cuh/.cu/.hpp/.h` 文件，统计缺失率
- [ ] 批量添加缺失的版权声明头
- [ ] 编写 `tools/check_copyright.py` 扫描工具
- [ ] 配置 `.licenserc.yaml` 声明豁免规则（third_party/ 等）
- [ ] 添加 CI workflow `license-check.yml`
- [ ] 更新 CONTRIBUTING.md，说明新文件须包含版权头

## 验收标准

- [ ] 所有 `.cpp/.cuh/.cu/.hpp/.h` 文件均包含 `SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0`
- [ ] 所有文件包含 `SPDX-FileCopyrightText` 声明
- [ ] CI 在缺少版权头时自动失败并给出明确报错
- [ ] PR checklist 包含版权声明确认项
- [ ] `third_party/` 目录豁免规则已配置，不产生误报
